# Transcrição: 07-Self Awareness Technique

## [00:00 - 03:07]

I mean, what's the difference between tricks and magic? Why am I saying magic like that? Magic. It's because magic is way better than tricks. So what are we talking about here? We're talking about what separates the good from the great. And in the world of magic, what separates the good from the great is self-awareness. Now, the same thing applies in the world of entrepreneurship. The entrepreneurs that do well are the entrepreneurs that are self-aware. And you'll find that in the world of communication, it's the exact same thing. Those communicators who are masterful, they are more self-aware. Now, there's a device that magicians use. And the device is this thing right here. Now, to the untrained eye, it can just look like a black pad. However, if you open this pad up, it's actually a three-way mirror. And if you look at me here, what you're going to start to notice is I use this particular pad to practice close-up magic. I use it to practice sleight of hand. And what I'm doing here is I'm building self-awareness on what people see all around me. This is how magicians are able to perform magic instead of tricks. Now, what separates it is simply this. A magician that performs tricks. The reason it's tricks is because people can kind of see behind the methodology. Oh, I saw you hide the card in your palm there. Ah, it's just a trick. However, I didn't know how until I came up with record and review. So the process is called record and review. And this is a very simple process. The first part of the process is you've got to film a five-minute video. You've got to film a five-minute video of yourself speaking. Now, when you're doing this, I'm quite specific with it. So make sure you do it standing up and make sure you just do it on your phone. And when you're doing this, talk about your day, talk about your favorite food, talk about something that you're passionate about. The content is not that critical. But make sure you get a whole five minutes. Don't just talk for a minute and go, oh, that's enough. No, no, no. Go for the full five. ## [03:07 - 06:08]

turn your phone around so you can't see yourself and then just listen. Because here you're doing an auditory review. So just listen and then listen out for the foundations. How was my rate of speech? Did I get stuck to a default rate of speech that after a while became monotonous even though it started as being very engaging? Take notes. Now you have self-awareness on this. Now you understand the value of rate of speech. Did you slow down on the important parts? And then did you just speed up over the bits that weren't as important? Did you do this? How about your volume? What was your default volume? Was it too quiet? Or were you too loud? Take notes, build extreme self-awareness. Pitch variety. How about tonality? Now the second time you reviewed, this time grab your phone. However, this time put your phone on mute. Then press play. Just look at yourself.
